summary: intel.json still claims the book is 100% cash, but broker_state.json confirms four fresh positions (UUUU, CAI, NTSK, YEXT) opened in the last ~75 minutes with zero hold time and stops not yet armed — that stale intel snapshot, plus unflagged post-lockup insider selling in NTSK and a possible DVLT ledger/broker desync on an already-avoided serial diluter, are the book's biggest live risks right now.

UUUU.note: Energy Fuels has an active $300M ATM (reinstated 2025-06-13, replacing a March-2024 agreement) — routine ongoing dilution mechanism for this size producer, not baby-shelf drip, but real; entry thesis is CEO Bhappu's $968k buy (7/7) and chairman Hansen's buy (7/8), yet Lorentzian k-NN reads -0.75 (consistency 0.75), a fairly strong bearish technical signal the insider-buy thesis doesn't address.

CAI.note: Fundamentals are clean — director bought $990k (7/13) and Jon Halbert bought 68,000 sh at $14.56 (5/15), Caris Detect launched commercially in early July, no new dilution filing found, and there's a $100M buyback (anti-dilutive); notched up from low because nightly data_quality.json flags CAI as broker_disagrees (broker $17.50 vs cache $16.99, 3.0% drift) on a HELD ticker — broker is the account of record so P&L should be fine, but confirm the cache refresh before trusting any stop math off cached price.

NTSK.note: Entry catalyst (fresh 52-wk high, Wells Fargo/Jefferies/Cantor Buy ratings this week) is real, but Netskope's March-2026 IPO lockup expiration has produced ongoing insider distribution — ~$18M of insider selling in the trailing 90 days, most recently major shareholder Lightspeed Venture Partners selling 10,621 shares on 2026-07-08, two days before this buy. That contradicts the desk's own "no negative news" read and should be watched, not ignored.

YEXT.note: No shelf/ATM/dilution filing found. CEO's $9/share take-private bid was withdrawn in Feb 2026 (financing fell through) and replaced with a self-tender buyback, since trimmed from $180M to $140M cap in March 2026 on macro/rate conditions — anti-dilutive and the failed-buyout news is already three-plus months old, not a fresh catalyst risk.

flags.why: Distressed reverse-merger pop, not organic strength: definitive Serapha Bio merger (6/22-6/23) plus $230M concurrent private placement leaves pre-merger Boundless holders only ~3.7% of the combined 'AATD' entity; own oncology pipeline halted, ~75% workforce cut. Unchanged this cycle, still on the desk's own avoid list.

10-Q discloses going-concern doubt including possible Chapter 7 liquidation; just closed a $23.7M private placement (warrant-overhang setup). Today's 43-64% intraday pop (per intel.json) has no confirmable fresh catalyst beyond the known trial/cash-runway headlines — treat as illiquid noise, not signal. FinBERT mean sentiment -0.288 today corroborates the cash-runway concern.

Live $100M ATM (since 2024) plus a Feb-2026 $107.6M underwritten offering (20M sh at $5.00), now underwater near $3.75. FinBERT mean -0.19 today. No new filing this cycle; well below buy threshold currently.
Automatic S-3ASR shelf effective 1/13/26, already used twice via 424B5, plus an active $50M ATM — ongoing drip-dilution setup. Well below buy threshold (score 1.83 on today's scan).

Radar's own daily-research notes flag DVLT as a live wildcard-sleeve position (18.28 sh on the ledger) that broker reconciliation now shows as ledger-only — broker no longer reports the position. DVLT is already on this desk's avoid list as a serial diluter; if it's actually still held anywhere, its P/L and any stop are being computed off a ledger price with no broker confirmation. This needs a human check before the next cycle, independent of the dilution call.

dilution_watch.note: $300M at-the-market equity distribution agreement (BMO/Canaccord/Cantor/B.Riley/H.C. Wainwright), replacing a March-2024 program — routine ongoing financing tool for this size producer, actively usable now.
Definitive Serapha Bio merger plus $230M concurrent private placement; existing holders diluted to ~3.7% pro forma.
$23.7M private placement ($8.5M upfront, up to $15.2M via Series G warrants); going-concern disclosure in most recent 10-Q.
$107.6M net underwritten offering (20M shares at $5.00), now underwater; $100M ATM (since 2024) remains active.
Automatic shelf effective, used twice via 424B5; $50M ATM active.

disagreements: intel.json's tape claims 'book is 100% cash ... no held-position risk right now' — this was stale even at generation time: broker_state.json (13:44:43Z, 12 min after intel's 13:32:14Z snapshot) confirms four live positions (UUUU, CAI, NTSK, YEXT) matching the ledger, all opened between 13:31 and 13:40 today. Intel should re-check broker state before declaring the book empty.

NTSK was bought and held clean per radar's daily-research notes ('no negative news'), but a major shareholder (Lightspeed Venture Partners) sold shares just two days before this buy (7/8) as part of ~$18M in trailing-90-day insider selling following the March-2026 lockup expiration — that's a real, if second-order, negative signal the desk missed.
